docs.title: Welcome to Oyez SA-ASR
docs.text: Oyez project scraper, for the SA-ASR task

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
# Clone from source
git clone git://github.com/AlonKellner/oyez_sa_asr
cd oyez_sa_asr

docs.title: HuggingFace Dataset Specifications
docs.text:
Edited by Claude

Detailed specifications for each dataset tier.

docs.title: Dataset 1: oyez-sa-asr-raw
docs.text:
Archive of original scraped files for reproducibility.

oyez-sa-asr-raw/
├── audio/{term}/{docket}/*.mp3, *.ogg
├── cases/{term}/{docket}.json
├── transcripts/{term}/{docket}.json
└── index/terms.json
Aspect Detail Audio Original MP3 + OGG (both preserved) Metadata Raw JSON from Oyez API Size ~340 GB Updates Append-only (yearly)

docs.title: Dataset 2: oyez-sa-asr-flex
docs.text:
Processed FLAC recordings with parquet metadata references. CLI: oyez dataset flex

oyez-sa-asr-flex/
├── audio/{term}/{docket}/*.flac   # 24-bit FLAC
└── data/
    ├── recordings.parquet         # Full recording metadata
    └── utterances.parquet         # Speaker turn refs (no audio)

docs.title: Configs
docs.text: Config Description Audio recordings Full oral arguments File refs utterances Speaker turns Sliced on load

docs.title: Utterances Schema
docs.text: Column Type Description term string Court term docket string Docket number transcript_type string argument/opinion start_sec float Start timestamp end_sec float End timestamp text string Transcript speaker_name string Speaker name speaker_id int Speaker ID

docs.title: Dataset 3: oyez-sa-asr-simple
docs.text:
Embedded audio for zero-friction streaming access. CLI: oyez dataset simple

oyez-sa-asr-simple/
├── oyez_sa_asr.py              # HuggingFace loading script
├── README.md                   # Dataset card
├── lt1m/data/utterances/       # < 1 minute utterances
├── lt5m/data/utterances/       # 1-5 minute utterances
└── lt30m/data/utterances/      # 5-30 minute utterances

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
from datasets import load_dataset

# Standard HuggingFace pattern
ds = load_dataset("path/to/datasets/simple", split="lt1m")
sample = ds[0]
print(sample["audio"]["array"])  # Decoded numpy array
print(sample["sentence"])        # Transcription

docs.title: Utterances Schema (HuggingFace-aligned)
docs.text: Column Type Description id string Unique utterance ID audio Audio Embedded FLAC bytes (auto-decoded) sentence string Transcript speaker string Speaker name duration float Duration in seconds term string Court term docket string Docket number start_sec float Start timestamp end_sec float End timestamp

docs.title: Why Larger (~2× FLAC)
docs.text:
• Arrow row metadata per utterance
  • Column value duplication
  • Less efficient chunking than standalone FLAC
  • Parquet index structures

docs.title: FLAC Seekability
docs.text:
FLAC supports efficient seeking via SEEKTABLE:

  • On-the-fly segment extraction
  • HTTP Range requests for cloud storage
  • Memory-efficient processing

docs.title: HuggingFace Dataset Versioning
docs.text:
Edited by Claude

Versioning strategy and annual release workflow.

docs.title: Version Naming
docs.text:
v{year}.{patch}

v2024.0  - Initial release with 2024 term
v2024.1  - Bug fix (transcript corrections)
v2025.0  - Add 2025 term

docs.title: Data Structure
docs.text:
Sharded by term for incremental updates:

data/utterances/
├── 1955.parquet
├── 1956.parquet
├── ...
└── 2024.parquet    # Added in v2024.0

docs.title: Loading Versions
docs.text:
# Latest
ds = load_dataset("org/oyez-sa-asr-flex")

# Pinned version
ds = load_dataset("org/oyez-sa-asr-flex", revision="v2023.0")

# Specific terms
ds = load_dataset("org/oyez-sa-asr-flex", "utterances",
                  data_files="data/utterances/202*.parquet")

docs.title: Term Configs
docs.text:
ds = load_dataset("org/oyez-sa-asr-flex", "all")     # All terms
ds = load_dataset("org/oyez-sa-asr-flex", "modern")  # 2000+
ds = load_dataset("org/oyez-sa-asr-flex", "recent")  # Last 5 terms
ds = load_dataset("org/oyez-sa-asr-flex", "2024")    # Single term

docs.title: Timeline
docs.text: Month Activity October Term's oral arguments complete November Scrape and process December QA and release

docs.title: Release Commands
docs.text:
# 1. Scrape & process new term
oyez scrape cases --term 2025
oyez scrape transcripts --term 2025
oyez scrape audio --term 2025
oyez process cases --term 2025
oyez process transcripts --term 2025
oyez process audio --term 2025

# 2. Create datasets
oyez dataset raw --term 2025
oyez dataset flex --term 2025
oyez dataset simple

# 3. Publish to HuggingFace
oyez publish raw --repo-id org/oyez-sa-asr-raw
oyez publish flex --repo-id org/oyez-sa-asr-flex
oyez publish simple --repo-id org/oyez-sa-asr-simple

docs.title: CHANGELOG Template
docs.text:
## [v2025.0] - 2025-12-01

### Added

- 2025 term: 72 arguments, 4,320 utterances

### Stats

- Recordings: 5,100 (+72)
- Utterances: 510,000 (+4,320)
- Storage: raw 345GB, flex 1.1TB, simple 2.2TB

docs.title: HuggingFace Dataset Architecture
docs.text:
Edited by Claude

Three-tier dataset architecture for Oyez Supreme Court oral arguments.

docs.title: Datasets Overview
docs.text:
Dataset CLI Command Purpose Size oyez-sa-asr-raw oyez dataset raw Archive of original MP3/OGG/JSON ~340 GB oyez-sa-asr-flex oyez dataset flex Processed FLAC + parquet refs ~1.1 TB oyez-sa-asr-simple oyez dataset simple Embedded audio for streaming ~2.2 TB

See detailed specifications in:

  • Dataset Specifications
  • Versioning & Releases

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text:
# Create datasets
oyez dataset raw --term 2024      # Package raw files
oyez dataset flex --term 2024     # Create FLAC + parquets
oyez dataset simple               # Embed audio (uses flex)

# Publish to HuggingFace
oyez publish raw --repo-id org/oyez-sa-asr-raw
oyez publish flex --repo-id org/oyez-sa-asr-flex
oyez publish simple --repo-id org/oyez-sa-asr-simple

from datasets import load_dataset

# Streaming - no local storage needed
ds = load_dataset("org/oyez-sa-asr-simple", "utterances", streaming=True)

# Full recordings with on-the-fly slicing
ds = load_dataset("org/oyez-sa-asr-flex", "recordings")

# Specific term
ds = load_dataset("org/oyez-sa-asr-flex", "2024")

# Pinned version
ds = load_dataset("org/oyez-sa-asr-flex", revision="v2024.0")

docs.title: Choosing the Right Dataset
docs.text: Use Case Dataset Config ASR training (quick start) oyez-sa-asr-simple utterances Custom preprocessing oyez-sa-asr-flex utterances Full recording transcription oyez-sa-asr-flex recordings Reproduce from scratch oyez-sa-asr-raw -

docs.title: Size & Growth
docs.text: Dataset Current Annual Growth oyez-sa-asr-raw ~340 GB +5-7 GB oyez-sa-asr-flex ~1.1 TB +15-20 GB oyez-sa-asr-simple ~2.2 TB +30-40 GB

docs.title: Oyez API Data Overview
docs.text:
Generated by Claude - January 2026

Summary of Oyez API structure and case data. See also: Audio Formats

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
• Total Cases: 8,477
  • Term Range: 1850-2025 (75 unique court terms)
  • Total Audio Recordings: 12,087

docs.title: Data Pipeline
docs.text:
scrape index  →  .cache/index/  →  process index  →  cases_index.json
                                                                    ↓
scrape cases     ←  reads case URLs  ←─────────────────────────────┘
     ↓
.cache/cases/api.oyez.org/raw/*.json  (8,477 case detail files)

docs.title: Case Data Structure
docs.text:
Each case JSON contains the following top-level fields:

Field Description ID Unique case identifier name Case name (e.g., "Roe v. Wade") href API URL for the case docket_number Court docket number term Supreme Court term (year) first_party / second_party Party names timeline List of case events with dates facts_of_the_case Factual background (HTML) question Legal question presented conclusion Court's conclusion advocates List of attorneys who argued oral_argument_audio Audio recordings of arguments opinion_announcement Audio of opinion announcements decisions Voting records with justice votes written_opinion Links to written opinions heard_by / decided_by Court composition

docs.title: 1. Oral Argument Audio
docs.text:
Recordings of attorneys and justices during oral arguments.

Metric Value Total recordings 8,570 Cases with audio 7,383 Cases with multiple arguments 1,026 Unavailable recordings 86

docs.title: 2. Opinion Announcement Audio
docs.text:
Recordings of justices reading opinions from the bench.

Metric Value Total recordings 3,517 Cases with audio 3,457

docs.title: Dissenting and Concurring Opinions
docs.text:
Justices may read separate opinions from the bench, recorded as multiple parts of the opinion announcement.

Example: Glossip v. Gross (2015) - 4 audio parts:

Part Speaker Role 1 Roberts Majority announcement 2 Sotomayor Dissent 3 Breyer Dissent 4 Scalia Concurrence

The opinion_type field in votes identifies authors: majority, dissent, concurrence, special concurrence, plurality, or none.

docs.title: Timeline Event Types
docs.text: Event Description Argued Oral argument date Decided Decision date Granted Certiorari granted Reargued Case reargued

docs.title: Decision Types
docs.text: Type Description majority opinion Most common per curiam Unsigned opinion plurality opinion No majority equally divided 4-4 split

docs.title: Vote Types
docs.text: Vote Count majority 61,845 minority 13,637 none 3,516

docs.title: Unavailable Audio
docs.text:
Some oral arguments are marked unavailable with public_note:

  • "The audio for this oral argument is missing"
  • "This case was dismissed before oral arguments"
  • "This case was settled before arguments"
  • "This argument audio is not available due to its exclusion from the National Archives"

docs.title: Argument 2.0 (argument2_url)
docs.text:
• 32 cases have links to argument2.oyez.org
  • This is a blog/analysis site, not additional audio

docs.title: Oyez Audio Formats & URLs
docs.text:
Generated by Claude - January 2026

Detailed audio file formats, transcript structure, and URL patterns. See also: API Overview

docs.title: Audio File Formats
docs.text:
Each recording is available in 3 formats:

Format MIME Type S3 Bucket MP3 audio/mpeg oyez.case-media.mp3 OGG audio/ogg oyez.case-media.ogg HLS application/x-mpegURL oyez.case-media.hls

docs.title: Audio File Naming Conventions
docs.text: Audio files follow specific naming patterns in S3:

docs.title: Oral Arguments
docs.text:
{docket}_{date}{suffix}.delivery.mp3
Example: 95-7452_19961104a.delivery.mp3

docs.title: Opinion Announcements
docs.text: Suffix Type -opinion Main/majority opinion -opinion-1, -opinion-2 Numbered parts -opinion-dissent Dissenting opinion -opinion-concur Concurring opinion

docs.title: Accessing Separate Opinion Audio
docs.text:
Data path:

Case JSON → opinion_announcement[] → href → case_media API → media_file[]

Title patterns identify opinion types:

Pattern Example Standard Opinion Announcement - June 30, 2023 Numbered Opinion Announcement - June 29, 2015 (Part 1) Dissent (new) Dissenting Opinion - Sotomayor - June 30, 2023 Dissent (old) Opinion Announcement - June 19, 2017 (Dissent by Breyer) Concurrence Concurring Opinion - Jackson - June 27, 2024

Example: 303 Creative v. Elenis (21-476):

{
  "opinion_announcement": [
    {
      "title": "Opinion Announcement - June 30, 2023",
      "href": "...opinion_announcement_audio/25574"
    },
    {
      "title": "Dissenting Opinion - Sotomayor - June 30, 2023",
      "href": "...opinion_announcement_audio/25583"
    }
  ]
}

Fetching the dissent href returns:

.../21-476_20230630-opinion-dissent.delivery.mp3

docs.title: Transcript Structure
docs.text:
The case_media response includes timestamped transcripts:

{
  "transcript": {
    "title": "Case Name",
    "duration": 3248.262,
    "sections": [
      {
        "start": 0,
        "stop": 1692.036,
        "turns": [
          {
            "start": 0,
            "stop": 9.747,
            "speaker": {
              "ID": 15141,
              "name": "William H. Rehnquist",
              "last_name": "Rehnquist"
            },
            "text_blocks": [
              {
                "start": 0,
                "stop": 8.658,
                "text": "The Court will hear argument now..."
              }
            ]
          }
        ]
      }
    ]
  }
}

docs.title: URL Patterns
docs.text: Resource URL Pattern Case list api.oyez.org/cases?page={n}&per_page={n} Case detail api.oyez.org/cases/{term}/{docket} Oral argument api.oyez.org/case_media/oral_argument_audio/{id} Opinion audio api.oyez.org/case_media/opinion_announcement_audio/{id}

docs.title: S3 Audio URLs
docs.text:
s3.amazonaws.com/oyez.case-media.mp3/case_data/{term}/{docket}/...
s3.amazonaws.com/oyez.case-media.ogg/case_data/{term}/{docket}/...
s3.amazonaws.com/oyez.case-media.hls/case_data/{term}/{docket}/...

docs.title: Written Opinion Types
docs.text: Type Count syllabus 7,948 case 6,627 majority 1,514 concurring 950 dissenting 948

docs.title: Next Steps for Audio ASR
docs.text:
To build an ASR dataset from this data:

  1. Extract audio URLs from cached case data
  2. Fetch case_media endpoints to get actual MP3/OGG URLs
  3. Download audio files from S3 buckets
  4. Extract transcripts from case_media responses
  5. Align audio with text using timestamp data

docs.title: Oyez Transcript Structure Analysis
docs.text:
Generated by Claude - January 2026

Analysis of 11,993 scraped transcript files from the Oyez case_media API.

docs.title: Overview Statistics
docs.text: Metric Value Total files 11,993 With transcript data 11,778 (98.2%) Without transcript 215 (1.8%) Damaged 1 Total audio duration 7,705 hours Average duration 39 min 15 sec

docs.title: Content Types
docs.text: Type Count Oral Arguments 8,297 Opinion Announcements 3,502 Dissenting Opinions 12 Concurring Opinions 2 Other 180

docs.title: JSON Structure
docs.text:
Each transcript response contains:

{
  "id": 12345,
  "title": "Oral Argument - March 21, 2023",
  "damaged": false,
  "unavailable": false,
  "public_note": null,
  "display_title": null,
  "media_file": [...],
  "transcript": {...}
}

docs.title: Media Files
docs.text:
Each response includes audio in 3 formats:

Format MIME Type Availability MP3 audio/mpeg 99.9% OGG audio/ogg 95.3% HLS application/x-mpegURL 95.3%

S3 URL pattern:

https://s3.amazonaws.com/oyez.case-media.{format}/case_data/{term}/{docket}/{filename}

docs.title: Transcript Object
docs.text:
{
  "transcript": {
    "title": "Case Name",
    "duration": 3248.262,
    "sections": [...]
  }
}

docs.title: Hierarchical Structure
docs.text:
transcript
  └── sections[] (1-291 per transcript)
        ├── start, stop (timestamps)
        ├── byte_start, byte_stop (audio file offsets)
        └── turns[] (1-647 per section)
              ├── start, stop (timestamps)
              ├── byte_start, byte_stop
              ├── speaker {...}
              └── text_blocks[] (1-185 per turn)
                    ├── start, stop (timestamps)
                    ├── byte_start, byte_stop
                    └── text (actual transcript text)

docs.title: Section Distribution
docs.text:
Sections Transcripts 1 4,012 (34%) 2 1,322 (11%) 3 3,957 (34%) 4 1,820 (15%) 5+ 667 (6%)

Most transcripts have 1-4 sections. One outlier has 291 sections.

docs.title: Totals
docs.text: Element Count Sections 31,449 Turns 1,843,327 Text Blocks 4,180,491

docs.title: Speaker Object
docs.text:
{
  "speaker": {
    "ID": 15086,
    "name": "John G. Roberts, Jr.",
    "last_name": "Roberts",
    "identifier": "john_g_roberts_jr",
    "href": "https://api.oyez.org/people/john_g_roberts_jr",
    "roles": [...],
    "thumbnail": {...}
  }
}

docs.title: Top Speakers by Turns
docs.text:
Justice Turns Byron R. White 81,702 Antonin Scalia 58,158 Felix Frankfurter 54,967 John Paul Stevens 51,463 William H. Rehnquist 51,412 Potter Stewart 50,640 Hugo L. Black 45,202

Unique speakers: 9,274 (includes justices, attorneys, and others)

docs.title: Null Speakers
docs.text:
89,761 turns (4.9%) have speaker: null.

These represent unattributed speech, typically:

  • Crosstalk or overlapping speech
  • Unclear attribution in older recordings
  • Audience reactions (laughter, etc.)

Example:

{
  "speaker": null,
  "text_blocks": [{"text": "0 [Generallaughter.]"}]
}

docs.title: Timestamp Anomalies
docs.text:
Negative durations: Some text blocks have stop < start:

Start: 2807.32s, Stop: 2781.44s (duration: -25.88s)
Start: 4939.75s, Stop: 0.00s (duration: -4939.75s)

This occurs in at least some transcripts due to:

  • Timestamp reset at section boundaries
  • Data entry errors in older transcripts

Zero duration: 6,191 text blocks (0.15%)

docs.title: Empty/Missing Data
docs.text:
• 6 empty text blocks (text is empty or whitespace)
  • 215 transcripts have no transcript data (older recordings)
  • 1 damaged recording marked explicitly

docs.title: Text Block Characteristics
docs.text:
Metric Value Min length 2 chars Max length 4,634 chars Average 107 chars Median 78 chars Average duration 6.5 seconds

Most text blocks are short utterances (< 150 chars).

docs.title: Data Filtering
docs.text:
1. Skip null transcripts (215 files)
  2. Skip damaged recordings
  3. Handle null speakers - decide whether to include or exclude

docs.title: Timestamp Handling
docs.text:
1. Validate durations - filter blocks with stop <= start
  2. Use byte offsets as fallback (100% present)
  3. Section boundaries - timestamps may reset per section

docs.title: Text Preprocessing
docs.text:
1. Remove annotations like [Generallaughter.]
  2. Handle empty blocks (6 total)
  3. Consider splitting very long blocks (>2000 chars)

docs.title: Audio-Text Alignment
docs.text:
The data provides multiple alignment options:

  • Timestamps (start/stop in seconds)
  • Byte offsets (byte_start/byte_stop for audio file)

Byte offsets are more reliable for older recordings.

docs.title: File Reference
docs.text: Analysis scripts: scripts/analyze_transcripts.py
